question: Does a separate, trace-feature VERIFIER (trained on semantic-entropy + neighbour-corroboration + author/source-confidence, with NO access to the gold answer) predict answer correctness BETTER than the existing provenance-grounded confidence prior (balanced accuracy ~0.52, non-monotonic per the failure ledger), AND does its calibration (ECE) stay flat-or-improving as the base agent scales up rather than degrading the way raw model confidence usually does?
constructs: correctness-discrimination (AUROC of the verifier's score vs binary answer correctness on held-out traces)
calibration-error (expected calibration error, ECE, of the verifier's probability vs empirical correctness)
scaling-monotonicity (sign of the ECE trend across >= 3 base-model sizes — does calibration get WORSE with scale, the usual failure, or hold/improve?)
delta-AUROC (learned trace-verifier minus provenance-confidence baseline; positive = the verifier adds signal beyond the source-quality prior)
delta-ECE (learned trace-verifier minus provenance-confidence baseline; negative = better-calibrated)
primaryMetric: delta AUROC of correctness prediction (trace-feature verifier minus provenance-confidence baseline), on a held-out, decontaminated trace set, paired per trace
secondaryMetrics: delta ECE (trace-verifier minus provenance-confidence baseline; guardrail: must not worsen calibration)
scaling-monotonicity: sign and magnitude of the ECE slope across >= 3 base sizes (the headline scaling claim)
risk-coverage / selective-accuracy at fixed coverage (does abstaining on low verifier-score traces raise answered accuracy?)
arms: provenance-confidence-baseline (the existing agent/grounded_confidence.py pooled source confidence — the current weak prior; REQUIRES the live grounded path, not run offline)
trace-feature-verifier (a small model/regressor over semantic_entropy + corroboration + authorConfidence trace features, trained on a disjoint split; the candidate)
raw-model-logprob-baseline (the base model's own sequence confidence — the usual overconfident-with-scale control to show the failure mode the verifier must beat)
primaryN: PENDING — requires a real, decontaminated trace corpus (agent question -> emitted answer -> gold-labelled correct/incorrect) with extracted trace features, split train/calibration/test, sized so MDE <= 0.05 on the AUROC scale at the test split (see primaryMDE). No real run has been performed; only the feature-extraction + scorer machinery is in-repo.

groundTruth.rule: answer correctness for each trace must be labelled by >= 2 INDEPENDENT judge families (judge != the answer agent, judge != the verifier being scored), reporting inter-judge agreement (kappa >= 0.40 OR Gwet AC1 with CI). AUROC/ECE are then computed deterministically from the verifier scores against those labels.
groundTruth.use: GO/NO-GO — author-only or self-judged correctness labels do NOT satisfy this; a verifier scored against its own training labels voids the run

noAnswerLeakage.rule: the verifier's input features are derived ONLY from the reasoning trace and retrieved sources, NEVER from the gold answer or the correctness label; the metamorphic feature audit tools/audit_feature_leakage.py (re-extract features after perturbing only the label/answer fields; any change = leakage) must PASS before any AUROC is reported. The current T3 extractor passes the audit.
noAnswerLeakage.use: GO/NO-GO — leaked-label features produce an inflated AUROC that is not a result

baselineContrast.rule: the provenance-confidence baseline AND the raw-logprob baseline are scored on the IDENTICAL held-out traces; the falsifier is that the cheap provenance prior already matches the learned verifier
baselineContrast.use: GO/NO-GO — without both baselines there is no delta to claim

calibrationGuardrail.rule: delta ECE must stay <= 0 (the verifier must not buy discrimination at the cost of worse calibration); a higher AUROC with worse ECE is not a win

scalingHonesty.rule: the scaling-monotonicity claim requires >= 3 genuinely different base sizes on the SAME traces; a 2-point trend is reported as illustrative only, never as the scaling result
scalingHonesty.use: GO/NO-GO for the scaling headline (the AUROC delta can still stand at a single size)

decontam.rule: test-split traces are disjoint from the verifier's training split AND from any base-model training/adapter corpus (tools/assert_decontam.py)

stoppingRule: anytime-valid confidence sequence (tools/eval_stats.confidence_sequence_mean) REQUIRED for any peeked metric; fixed-n paired bootstrap CI (tools/eval_stats.bootstrap_ci_paired) only at the pre-specified test-split N
decontam: trace corpus held disjoint across train/calibration/test and from base-model training (tools/assert_decontam.py); any synthetic seed traces are committed but excluded from training rows (tools/lint_training_rows.py)
magnitudeRule: GO requires delta AUROC >= +0.05 with a 95% CI that EXCLUDES 0, AND delta ECE <= 0; the scaling headline additionally requires a non-positive ECE slope across >= 3 base sizes with a CI excluding a positive slope
ranking: guardrails first (no answer-leakage, both baselines present, calibration guardrail), then primary delta AUROC, then delta ECE, then the scaling-monotonicity trend
honestLimits: the failure ledger already establishes the provenance confidence is a WEAK, non-monotonic predictor of correctness (balanced accuracy ~0.52); this experiment's whole point is to beat that bar, and a NULL/NEGATIVE (the learned verifier does NOT beat the cheap prior) is a valid, publishable outcome — log it, do not tune to GO

AUROC measures discrimination, not deployment safety; a verifier that ranks correct-above-incorrect can still be miscalibrated in absolute probability (hence ECE is a co-primary guardrail, not a footnote)

trace features (semantic entropy, corroboration, author confidence) are themselves proxies; a verifier that learns them can inherit their blind spots — out-of-distribution traces are explicitly out of scope of any claim here
the scaling claim is the hardest part: it needs >= 3 base sizes evaluated on identical traces, which is GPU-bound and must go through the GitHub Actions cost-guarded path, never local SSH
no real run has been performed; the committed artifact is preregistration_only and the verdict is NO-GO until the harness, the decontaminated trace corpus, and >= 2 judge families exist

enforcement.harness: tools/run_calibration_verifier_eval.py — extracts trace features from agent/semantic_entropy.py + agent/corroboration.py, fits the verifier on a disjoint split, scores three arms, emits AUROC/ECE + paired-bootstrap delta CI; --mock exercises the math on synthetic traces, --traces scores a REAL generated corpus (per-model AUROC/ECE across the >=3-size scaling axis + ECE slope), --emit-pending writes the not-run artifact, --model refuses
enforcement.gpuPipeline: tools/run_calibration_traces.py generates the corpus (K answer samples + retrieval evidence + self-confidence + gold-match label per question across the model list); the mock backend is the OFFLINE smoke and the hf backend is the real pod generation. Dispatched via .github/workflows/calibration-verifier-runpod.yml (RunPod, task=calibration in tools/runpod_rlvr.py; offline smoke default, always-delete pod). The workflow must be on the default branch to be dispatchable.
enforcement.leakageAudit: tools/audit_feature_leakage.py — metamorphic no-answer-leakage audit (the T3 extractor passes; tests/test_audit_feature_leakage.py)
enforcement.judgePanel: tools/run_judge_panel.py — multi-judge labeling panel: majority consensus + pairwise Cohen kappa / Gwet AC1 (+ bootstrap CI) + the >= 0.40 gate, the instrument that turns single-source labels into >= 2-family ground truth (tests/test_run_judge_panel.py)
enforcement.stats: tools/eval_stats.py — paired bootstrap CI (fixed-n) + anytime-valid confidence sequence (peeked) + mde_at_n power check on the AUROC delta + cohen_kappa/gwet_ac1 for the label panel
enforcement.tests: tests/test_calibration_verifier_eval.py: AUROC/ECE math on a deterministic mock, the delta+CI logic, the real leakage-audit pillar, and the not-run/NO-GO pending artifact
enforcement.lint_claims: tools/lint_claims.py — no-overclaim copy must hold for any prose that cites this result
enforcement.decontam: tools/assert_decontam.py — trace-split disjointness + base-corpus disjointness before any real run
enforcement.note: PRE-REGISTRATION ONLY. This is the T3 thesis from docs/09-Agent/notes/DiveIntoLLMs-Takeaways.md — the highest-leverage open calibration item. No model run has been performed; the claim is never auto-promoted and canClaimAGI stays false.
